'''Tsushimazuka Kofun''' (対馬塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市|12=draft}}, Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|12=draft}}. Its shape is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}. It is one of the kofun that constitute the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|12=draft}}, designated as a National Historic Site of Japan.
It is the second-largest keyhole-shaped kofun on Iki Island after the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|12=draft}}.<ref name="Iki Tourism Navi Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.ikikankou.com/wp/?p=3831 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Iki City Tourism Federation "Iki Tourism Navi").</ref> It is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Overview ==
The kofun was constructed on a hill about 100 meters above sea level in the central part of Iki Island. The name "Tsushimazuka" is believed to derive from the view of {{ill|Tsushima Island|ja|Tsushima Province|12=draft}} from the mound.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/240 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Education Bureau, "Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Encyclopedia").</ref> Investigations were conducted in the early Showa period by Tomoo Matsumoto, in 1981 by Yasuhiko Matsunaga, and in 1991 by the {{ill|Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|ja|長崎県教育委員会|12=draft}}. Extensive excavations were carried out between 1997 and 2000.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Mound ==
The mound is keyhole-shaped with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * **Total Length:** About 65 meters * **Rear Circular Mound:** Approximately 36 meters in diameter, about 8 meters in height * **Front Rectangular Mound:** Approximately 25 meters wide
Similar to the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|12=draft}}, the front part of the mound is longer compared to the rear, which has steep slopes resembling an overturned bowl. The top of the rear mound was flattened during wartime to install anti-aircraft guns, giving the appearance of a two-tiered structure today. No evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} (covering stones) or surrounding moats has been found. A small shrine is currently enshrined at the base of the front part of the mound.
== Burial Facility ==
The burial facility consists of a dual-chamber {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Historical records indicate that the stone chamber had already been opened during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}} and people could enter it{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
**Stone Chamber Dimensions:** * **Total Length:** 9.4 meters{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (11 meters<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>) * **Main Chamber:** Approximately 3.6 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 3.8 meters high * **Antechamber:** Approximately 3.9 meters long, 1.5 meters wide
== Artifacts ==
Artifacts discovered include gold items, gilt-bronze sword hilts, horse gear, dragonfly beads, pottery, and {{ill|Silla pottery|ja|新羅土器|12=draft}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
<gallery> Tsushimazuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|Top of the rear circular mound </gallery>

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|双六古墳|" to "|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|"
revision.text:
{{Infobox Kofun of Japan | Name = Tsushimazuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tsushimazuka Kofun, hokora.jpg|280px]] | Caption = Front part of the mound with stone shrine | Other Name = | Cluster = {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|12=draft}} | Location = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|12=draft}} {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市|12=draft}} Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi | Latitude = 33|47|43.02 | Longitude = 129|41|31.62 | ISO = JP-42 | Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} | Size = 65m in length, 8m in height | Burial = Dual-chamber {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} | Artifacts = Gold items, gilt-bronze sword hilt, horse gear, dragonfly beads, pottery, Silla pottery | Satellite Mounds = | Built = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}} | Burial Person = | Historic Site = Part of the National Historic Site "{{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|12=draft}}" | Tangible Cultural Property = | Notes = | Map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Keyhole-shaped kofun-south | Label = Tsushimazuka Kofun | Label Position = bottom }}
'''Tsushimazuka Kofun''' (対馬塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市|12=draft}}, Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|12=draft}}. Its shape is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}. It is one of the kofun that constitute the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|12=draft}}, designated as a National Historic Site of Japan.
It is the second-largest keyhole-shaped kofun on Iki Island after the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|12=draft}}.<ref name="Iki Tourism Navi Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.ikikankou.com/wp/?p=3831 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Iki City Tourism Federation "Iki Tourism Navi").</ref> It is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Overview ==
The kofun was constructed on a hill about 100 meters above sea level in the central part of Iki Island. The name "Tsushimazuka" is believed to derive from the view of {{ill|Tsushima Island|ja|Tsushima Province|12=draft}} from the mound.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/240 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Education Bureau, "Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Encyclopedia").</ref> Investigations were conducted in the early Showa period by Tomoo Matsumoto, in 1981 by Yasuhiko Matsunaga, and in 1991 by the {{ill|Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|ja|長崎県教育委員会|12=draft}}. Extensive excavations were carried out between 1997 and 2000.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Mound ==
The mound is keyhole-shaped with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * '''Total Length:''' About 65 meters * '''Rear Circular Mound:''' Approximately 36 meters in diameter, about 8 meters in height * '''Front Rectangular Mound:''' Approximately 25 meters wide
Similar to the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|12=draft}}, the front part of the mound is longer compared to the rear, which has steep slopes resembling an overturned bowl. The top of the rear mound was flattened during wartime to install anti-aircraft guns, giving the appearance of a two-tiered structure today. No evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} (covering stones) or surrounding moats has been found. A small shrine is currently enshrined at the base of the front part of the mound.
== Burial Facility ==
The burial facility consists of a dual-chamber {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Historical records indicate that the stone chamber had already been opened during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}} and people could enter it{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
'''Stone Chamber Dimensions:''' * '''Total Length:''' 9.4 meters{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (11 meters<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>) * '''Main Chamber:''' Approximately 3.6 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 3.8 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' Approximately 3.9 meters long, 1.5 meters wide
== Artifacts ==
Artifacts discovered include gold items, gilt-bronze sword hilts, horse gear, dragonfly beads, pottery, and {{ill|Silla pottery|ja|新羅土器|12=draft}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
<gallery> Tsushimazuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|Top of the rear circular mound </gallery>
== Related Facilities ==
* {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|12=draft}} (Located in Ashibe Town, Iki City)
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used in writing the article)}} * Published by local governments: ''' {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2017|chapter=|title=Iki City's Cultural Properties|publisher=Iki City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iki City's Cultural Properties|2017}}}} * Encyclopedias: ''' {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=Hajime Tagawa|year=2002|chapter=Tsushimazuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}}
== Further Reading == {{small|(Related literature not used in writing the article)}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/14748 |title=Detailed Distribution Survey Report of Prefectural Kofun - A Report on Mound and Stone Chamber Documentation-|series=Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Property Survey Report No. 106|publisher=Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=|title=Tsushimazuka Kofun - Excavation Survey in Connection with City Ruins Research Project-|series=Iki City Cultural Property Survey Report No. 6|publisher=Iki City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=|title=Ancient Tombs of Iki Island - Current Status Survey-|series=Iki City Cultural Property Survey Report No. 20|publisher=Iki City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}}

'''Tsushimazuka Kofun''' (対馬塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市|12=draft}}, Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|12=draft}}. Its shape is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}. It is one of the kofun that constitute the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|12=draft}}, designated as a National Historic Site of Japan.
It is the second-largest keyhole-shaped kofun on Iki Island after the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|12=draft}}.<ref name="Iki Tourism Navi Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.ikikankou.com/wp/?p=3831 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Iki City Tourism Federation "Iki Tourism Navi").</ref> It is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Overview ==
The kofun was constructed on a hill about 100 meters above sea level in the central part of Iki Island. The name "Tsushimazuka" is believed to derive from the view of {{ill|Tsushima Island|ja|Tsushima Province|12=draft}} from the mound.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/240 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Education Bureau, "Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Encyclopedia").</ref> Investigations were conducted in the early Showa period by Tomoo Matsumoto, in 1981 by Yasuhiko Matsunaga, and in 1991 by the {{ill|Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|ja|長崎県教育委員会|12=draft}}. Extensive excavations were carried out between 1997 and 2000.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Mound ==
The mound is keyhole-shaped with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * '''Total Length:''' About 65 meters * '''Rear Circular Mound:''' Approximately 36 meters in diameter, about 8 meters in height * '''Front Rectangular Mound:''' Approximately 25 meters wide
Similar to the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|12=draft}}, the front part of the mound is longer compared to the rear, which has steep slopes resembling an overturned bowl. The top of the rear mound was flattened during wartime to install anti-aircraft guns, giving the appearance of a two-tiered structure today. No evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} (covering stones) or surrounding moats has been found. A small shrine is currently enshrined at the base of the front part of the mound.
== Burial Facility ==
The burial facility consists of a dual-chamber {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Historical records indicate that the stone chamber had already been opened during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}} and people could enter it{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
'''Stone Chamber Dimensions:''' * '''Total Length:''' 9.4 meters{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (11 meters<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>) * '''Main Chamber:''' Approximately 3.6 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 3.8 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' Approximately 3.9 meters long, 1.5 meters wide
== Artifacts ==
Artifacts discovered include gold items, gilt-bronze sword hilts, horse gear, dragonfly beads, pottery, and {{ill|Silla pottery|ja|新羅土器|12=draft}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
<gallery> Tsushimazuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|Top of the rear circular mound </gallery>
== Related Facilities ==
* {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|12=draft}} (Located in Ashibe Town, Iki City)
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used in writing the article)}} * Published by local governments: ''' {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2017|chapter=|title=Iki City's Cultural Properties|publisher=Iki City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iki City's Cultural Properties|2017}}}} * Encyclopedias: ''' {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=Hajime Tagawa|year=2002|chapter=Tsushimazuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}}
== Further Reading == {{small|(Related literature not used in writing the article)}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/14748 |title=Detailed Distribution Survey Report of Prefectural Kofun - A Report on Mound and Stone Chamber Documentation-|series=Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Property Survey Report No. 106|publisher=Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=|title=Tsushimazuka Kofun - Excavation Survey in Connection with City Ruins Research Project-|series=Iki City Cultural Property Survey Report No. 6|publisher=Iki City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=|title=Ancient Tombs of Iki Island - Current Status Survey-|series=Iki City Cultural Property Survey Report No. 20|publisher=Iki City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}}

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox Kofun of Japan | Name = Tsushimazuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tsushimazuka Kofun, hokora.jpg|280px]] | Caption = Front part of the mound with stone shrine | Other Name = | Cluster = {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|12=draft}} | Location = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|12=draft}} {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市|12=draft}} Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi | Latitude = 33|47|43.02 | Longitude = 129|41|31.62 | ISO = JP-42 | Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} | Size = 65m in length, 8m in height | Burial = Dual-chamber {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} | Artifacts = Gold items, gilt-bronze sword hilt, horse gear, dragonfly beads, pottery, Silla pottery | Satellite Mounds = | Built = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}} | Burial Person = | Historic Site = Part of the National Historic Site "{{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|12=draft}}" | Tangible Cultural Property = | Notes = | Map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Keyhole-shaped kofun-south | Label = Tsushimazuka Kofun | Label Position = bottom }}
'''Tsushimazuka Kofun''' (対馬塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市|12=draft}}, Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|12=draft}}. Its shape is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}. It is one of the kofun that constitute the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|12=draft}}, designated as a National Historic Site of Japan.
It is the second-largest keyhole-shaped kofun on Iki Island after the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|12=draft}}.<ref name="Iki Tourism Navi Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.ikikankou.com/wp/?p=3831 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Iki City Tourism Federation "Iki Tourism Navi").</ref> It is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Overview ==
The kofun was constructed on a hill about 100 meters above sea level in the central part of Iki Island. The name "Tsushimazuka" is believed to derive from the view of {{ill|Tsushima Island|ja|Tsushima Province|12=draft}} from the mound.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/240 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Education Bureau, "Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Encyclopedia").</ref> Investigations were conducted in the early Showa period by Tomoo Matsumoto, in 1981 by Yasuhiko Matsunaga, and in 1991 by the {{ill|Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|ja|長崎県教育委員会|12=draft}}. Extensive excavations were carried out between 1997 and 2000.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Mound ==
The mound is keyhole-shaped with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * '''Total Length:''' About 65 meters * '''Rear Circular Mound:''' Approximately 36 meters in diameter, about 8 meters in height * '''Front Rectangular Mound:''' Approximately 25 meters wide
Similar to the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|12=draft}}, the front part of the mound is longer compared to the rear, which has steep slopes resembling an overturned bowl. The top of the rear mound was flattened during wartime to install anti-aircraft guns, giving the appearance of a two-tiered structure today. No evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} (covering stones) or surrounding moats has been found. A small shrine is currently enshrined at the base of the front part of the mound.
== Burial Facility ==
The burial facility consists of a dual-chamber {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Historical records indicate that the stone chamber had already been opened during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}} and people could enter it{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
'''Stone Chamber Dimensions:''' * '''Total Length:''' 9.4 meters{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (11 meters<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>) * '''Main Chamber:''' Approximately 3.6 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 3.8 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' Approximately 3.9 meters long, 1.5 meters wide
== Artifacts ==
Artifacts discovered include gold items, gilt-bronze sword hilts, horse gear, dragonfly beads, pottery, and {{ill|Silla pottery|ja|新羅土器|12=draft}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
<gallery> Tsushimazuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|Top of the rear circular mound </gallery>
== Related Facilities ==
* {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|12=draft}} (Located in Ashibe Town, Iki City)
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used in writing the article)}} * Published by local governments: ''' {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2017|chapter=|title=Iki City's Cultural Properties|publisher=Iki City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iki City's Cultural Properties|2017}}}} * Encyclopedias: ''' {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=Hajime Tagawa|year=2002|chapter=Tsushimazuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}}
== Further Reading == {{small|(Related literature not used in writing the article)}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/14748 |title=Detailed Distribution Survey Report of Prefectural Kofun - A Report on Mound and Stone Chamber Documentation-|series=Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Property Survey Report No. 106|publisher=Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=|title=Tsushimazuka Kofun - Excavation Survey in Connection with City Ruins Research Project-|series=Iki City Cultural Property Survey Report No. 6|publisher=Iki City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=|title=Ancient Tombs of Iki Island - Current Status Survey-|series=Iki City Cultural Property Survey Report No. 20|publisher=Iki City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}}

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 対馬塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Tsushimazuka Kofun, hokora.jpg|280px]] |画像キャプション = 前方部前面・石祠 |別名 = |所属 = [[壱岐古墳群]] |所在地 = [[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]勝本町立石東触 |緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 47|緯度秒 = 43.02 |経度度 = 129|経度分 = 41|経度秒 = 31.62 |ISO = JP-42 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長65m
高さ8m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = 金製品・金銅製大刀柄頭・馬具類・トンボ玉・陶器・新羅土器 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「壱岐古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nagasaki |アイコン = 前方後円墳-南 |ラベル = 対馬塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} '''対馬塚古墳'''（つしまづかこふん）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]勝本町立石東触にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[壱岐古墳群]]（国の[[史跡]]）を構成する古墳の1つ。 壱岐島では双六古墳に次ぐ第2位の規模の前方後円墳で[http://www.ikikankou.com/wp/?p=3831 対馬塚古墳]（壱岐市観光連盟「壱岐観光ナビ」）。、6世紀後半頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|対馬塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 == 概要 == 壱岐島中央部、標高100メートルの丘陵上に築造された古墳である。「対馬塚」の名は[[対馬島]]を見渡せることが由来と見られる[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/240 対馬塚古墳]（長崎県教育庁学芸文化課「長崎県の遺跡大辞典」）。。昭和初期に松本友雄による調査が、[[1981年]]（昭和56年）に松永泰彦による調査が、[[1991年]]（平成3年）に[[長崎県教育委員会]]による墳丘・石室の実測調査がなされたほか{{Sfn|対馬塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}、[[1997年]]-[[2000年]]（平成9年-12年）にも調査がなされている。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘は前方後円形で、規模は次の通り{{Sfn|対馬塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * 墳丘長：約65メートル * 後円部：直径約36メートル、高さ約8メートル * 前方部：幅約25メートル 双六古墳同様、墳丘の前方部は後円部に比して長く、後円部はお椀を伏せたような急斜面をなす特徴を有している{{Sfn|対馬塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。戦時中に高射砲を据えるため後円部墳頂が削平され、途中に段も付されており、現在では2段築成かのような外見となっている{{Sfn|対馬塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。他の外部施設として、[[葺石]]や周濠は認められていない{{Sfn|対馬塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。現在は前方部の裾部に祠が祀られている{{Sfn|対馬塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 == 埋葬施設 == 埋葬施設は複室構造の両袖式[[横穴式石室]]で、玄室・前室の2室と羨道で構成される{{Sfn|対馬塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。史料によると、[[江戸時代]]にはすでに石室は開口し人が出入りしていたという{{Sfn|対馬塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|対馬塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * 石室全長：9.4メートル{{Sfn|対馬塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}（11メートル） * 玄室：長さ約3.6メートル、幅約2.4メートル、高さ約3.8メートル * 前室：長さ約3.9メートル、幅約1.5メートル == 出土品 == 出土品としては、金製品・金銅製大刀柄頭・馬具類・トンボ玉・陶器・新羅土器が見つかっている。 Tsushimazuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|後円部墳頂 == 関連施設 == * [[壱岐市立一支国博物館]]（壱岐市芦辺町深江鶴亀触） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2017|chapter=|title=壱岐市の文化財|publisher=壱岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|壱岐市の文化財|2017}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=田川肇|year=2002|chapter=対馬塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|対馬塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/14748 |title=県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書 -県内古墳の墳丘・石室の資料化に伴う報告書-|series=長崎県文化財調査報告書第106集|publisher=長崎県教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=|title=対馬塚古墳 -市内遺跡発掘調査事業に伴う発掘調査-|series=壱岐市文化財調査報告書第6集|publisher=壱岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=|title=壱岐の島の古墳群 -現状調査-|series=壱岐市文化財調査報告書第20集|publisher=壱岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Tsushimazuka Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 壱岐古墳群] - 長崎県ホームページ「長崎県の文化財」 {{デフォルトソート:つしまつかこふん}} [[Category:壱岐古墳群]] [[Category:長崎県の古墳]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:長崎県にある国指定の史跡]]

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 対馬塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Tsushimazuka Kofun, hokora.jpg|280px]] |画像キャプション = 前方部前面・石祠 |別名 = |所属 = [[壱岐古墳群]] |所在地 = [[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]勝本町立石東触 |緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 47|緯度秒 = 43.02 |経度度 = 129|経度分 = 41|経度秒 = 31.62 |ISO = JP-42 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長65m
高さ8m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = 金製品・金銅製大刀柄頭・馬具類・トンボ玉・陶器・新羅土器 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「壱岐古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nagasaki |アイコン = 前方後円墳-南 |ラベル = 対馬塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} '''対馬塚古墳'''（つしまづかこふん）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]勝本町立石東触にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[壱岐古墳群]]（国の[[史跡]]）を構成する古墳の1つ。 壱岐島では双六古墳に次ぐ第2位の規模の前方後円墳で[http://www.ikikankou.com/wp/?p=3831 対馬塚古墳]（壱岐市観光連盟「壱岐観光ナビ」）。、6世紀後半頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|対馬塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 == 概要 == 壱岐島中央部、標高100メートルの丘陵上に築造された古墳である。「対馬塚」の名は[[対馬島]]を見渡せることが由来と見られる[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/240 対馬塚古墳]（長崎県教育庁学芸文化課「長崎県の遺跡大辞典」）。。昭和初期に松本友雄による調査が、[[1981年]]（昭和56年）に松永泰彦による調査が、[[1991年]]（平成3年）に[[長崎県教育委員会]]による墳丘・石室の実測調査がなされたほか{{Sfn|対馬塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}、[[1997年]]-[[2000年]]（平成9年-12年）にも調査がなされている。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘は前方後円形で、規模は次の通り{{Sfn|対馬塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * 墳丘長：約65メートル * 後円部：直径約36メートル、高さ約8メートル * 前方部：幅約25メートル 双六古墳同様、墳丘の前方部は後円部に比して長く、後円部はお椀を伏せたような急斜面をなす特徴を有している{{Sfn|対馬塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。戦時中に高射砲を据えるため後円部墳頂が削平され、途中に段も付されており、現在では2段築成かのような外見となっている{{Sfn|対馬塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。他の外部施設として、[[葺石]]や周濠は認められていない{{Sfn|対馬塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。現在は前方部の裾部に祠が祀られている{{Sfn|対馬塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 == 埋葬施設 == 埋葬施設は複室構造の両袖式[[横穴式石室]]で、玄室・前室の2室と羨道で構成される{{Sfn|対馬塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。史料によると、[[江戸時代]]にはすでに石室は開口し人が出入りしていたという{{Sfn|対馬塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|対馬塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * 石室全長：9.4メートル{{Sfn|対馬塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}（11メートル） * 玄室：長さ約3.6メートル、幅約2.4メートル、高さ約3.8メートル * 前室：長さ約3.9メートル、幅約1.5メートル == 出土品 == 出土品としては、金製品・金銅製大刀柄頭・馬具類・トンボ玉・陶器・新羅土器が見つかっている。 Tsushimazuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|後円部墳頂 == 関連施設 == * [[壱岐市立一支国博物館]]（壱岐市芦辺町深江鶴亀触） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2017|chapter=|title=壱岐市の文化財|publisher=壱岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|壱岐市の文化財|2017}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=田川肇|year=2002|chapter=対馬塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|対馬塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/14748 |title=県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書 -県内古墳の墳丘・石室の資料化に伴う報告書-|series=長崎県文化財調査報告書第106集|publisher=長崎県教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=|title=対馬塚古墳 -市内遺跡発掘調査事業に伴う発掘調査-|series=壱岐市文化財調査報告書第6集|publisher=壱岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=|title=壱岐の島の古墳群 -現状調査-|series=壱岐市文化財調査報告書第20集|publisher=壱岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Tsushimazuka Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 壱岐古墳群] - 長崎県ホームページ「長崎県の文化財」 {{デフォルトソート:つしまつかこふん}} [[Category:壱岐古墳群]] [[Category:長崎県の古墳]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:長崎県にある国指定の史跡]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Kofun of Japan | Name = Tsushimazuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tsushimazuka Kofun, hokora.jpg|280px]] | Caption = Front part of the mound with stone shrine | Other Name = | Cluster = {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Location = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi | Latitude = 33|47|43.02 | Longitude = 129|41|31.62 | ISO = JP-42 | Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Size = 65m in length, 8m in height | Burial = Dual-chamber {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Artifacts = Gold items, gilt-bronze sword hilt, horse gear, dragonfly beads, pottery, Silla pottery | Satellite Mounds = | Built = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Burial Person = | Historic Site = Part of the National Historic Site "{{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" | Tangible Cultural Property = | Notes = | Map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Keyhole-shaped kofun-south | Label = Tsushimazuka Kofun | Label Position = bottom }}
'''Tsushimazuka Kofun''' (対馬塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its shape is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is one of the kofun that constitute the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, designated as a National Historic Site of Japan.
It is the second-largest keyhole-shaped kofun on Iki Island after the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="Iki Tourism Navi Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.ikikankou.com/wp/?p=3831 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Iki City Tourism Federation "Iki Tourism Navi").</ref> It is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Overview ==
The kofun was constructed on a hill about 100 meters above sea level in the central part of Iki Island. The name "Tsushimazuka" is believed to derive from the view of {{ill|Tsushima Island|ja|Tsushima Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from the mound.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/240 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Education Bureau, "Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Encyclopedia").</ref> Investigations were conducted in the early Showa period by Tomoo Matsumoto, in 1981 by Yasuhiko Matsunaga, and in 1991 by the {{ill|Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|ja|長崎県教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Extensive excavations were carried out between 1997 and 2000.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Mound ==
The mound is keyhole-shaped with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * '''Total Length:''' About 65 meters * '''Rear Circular Mound:''' Approximately 36 meters in diameter, about 8 meters in height * '''Front Rectangular Mound:''' Approximately 25 meters wide
Similar to the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the front part of the mound is longer compared to the rear, which has steep slopes resembling an overturned bowl. The top of the rear mound was flattened during wartime to install anti-aircraft guns, giving the appearance of a two-tiered structure today. No evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (covering stones) or surrounding moats has been found. A small shrine is currently enshrined at the base of the front part of the mound.
== Burial Facility ==
The burial facility consists of a dual-chamber {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Historical records indicate that the stone chamber had already been opened during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and people could enter it{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
'''Stone Chamber Dimensions:''' * '''Total Length:''' 9.4 meters{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (11 meters<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>) * '''Main Chamber:''' Approximately 3.6 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 3.8 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' Approximately 3.9 meters long, 1.5 meters wide
== Artifacts ==
Artifacts discovered include gold items, gilt-bronze sword hilts, horse gear, dragonfly beads, pottery, and {{ill|Silla pottery|ja|新羅土器|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
<gallery> Tsushimazuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|Top of the rear circular mound </gallery>
== Related Facilities ==
* {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Located in Ashibe Town, Iki City)
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used in writing the article)}} * Published by local governments: ''' {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2017|chapter=|title=Iki City's Cultural Properties|publisher=Iki City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iki City's Cultural Properties|2017}}}} * Encyclopedias: ''' {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=Hajime Tagawa|year=2002|chapter=Tsushimazuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}}
== Further Reading == {{small|(Related literature not used in writing the article)}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/14748 |title=Detailed Distribution Survey Report of Prefectural Kofun - A Report on Mound and Stone Chamber Documentation-|series=Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Property Survey Report No. 106|publisher=Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=|title=Tsushimazuka Kofun - Excavation Survey in Connection with City Ruins Research Project-|series=Iki City Cultural Property Survey Report No. 6|publisher=Iki City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=|title=Ancient Tombs of Iki Island - Current Status Survey-|series=Iki City Cultural Property Survey Report No. 20|publisher=Iki City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsushimazuka Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Cluster] - Nagasaki Prefecture's official website on cultural properties * [[Sugoroku Kofun]] ...meters; the largest in Iki Islan ...<nowiki>{{ill|Tsushima-tsuka Kofun|ja|'''対馬塚古墳'''|qq=draft|qq=draft}}</nowiki>, followed by {{ill|Sugoroku Kofun|ja|'''双六古墳'''<nowiki>|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and l ...ngth: 91 meters; the largest in Iki Islan ...{{ill|Tsushima-tsuka Kofun|ja|'''対馬塚古墳'''|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, followed by {{ill|Sugoroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and later transitioned to ci ... 784 bytes (100 words) - 06:28, 19 January 2025 * [[Iki Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Tsushima-tsuka Kofun|ja|'''対馬塚古墳'''|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Keyhole-shaped mound (total length: 63 meters). ...fun Group began in the late 6th century with {{ill|Tsushima-tsuka Kofun|ja|'''対馬塚古墳'''|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, followed by {{ill|Sugoroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and later transitioned to ci ...

No evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (covering stones) or surrounding moats has been found. A small shrine is currently enshrined at the base of the front part of the mound.

The burial facility consists of a dual-chamber {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Historical records indicate that the stone chamber had already been opened during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and people could enter it{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
'''Stone Chamber Dimensions:''' * '''Total Length:''' 9.4 meters{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (11 meters<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>) * '''Main Chamber:''' Approximately 3.6 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 3.8 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' Approximately 3.9 meters long, 1.5 meters wide
== Artifacts ==
Artifacts discovered include gold items, gilt-bronze sword hilts, horse gear, dragonfly beads, pottery, and {{ill|Silla pottery|ja|新羅土器|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
<gallery> Tsushimazuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|Top of the rear circular mound </gallery>
== Related Facilities ==
* {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Located in Ashibe Town, Iki City)
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used in writing the article)}} * Published by local governments: ''' {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2017|chapter=|title=Iki City's Cultural Properties|publisher=Iki City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iki City's Cultural Properties|2017}}}} * Encyclopedias: ''' {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=Hajime Tagawa|year=2002|chapter=Tsushimazuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox Kofun of Japan | Name = Tsushimazuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tsushimazuka Kofun, hokora.jpg|280px]] | Caption = Front part of the mound with stone shrine | Other Name = | Cluster = {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Location = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi | Latitude = 33|47|43.02 | Longitude = 129|41|31.62 | ISO = JP-42 | Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Size = 65m in length, 8m in height | Burial = Dual-chamber {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Artifacts = Gold items, gilt-bronze sword hilt, horse gear, dragonfly beads, pottery, Silla pottery | Satellite Mounds = | Built = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Burial Person = | Historic Site = Part of the National Historic Site "{{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" | Tangible Cultural Property = | Notes = | Map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Keyhole-shaped kofun-south | Label = Tsushimazuka Kofun | Label Position = bottom }}
'''Tsushimazuka Kofun''' (対馬塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its shape is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is one of the kofun that constitute the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, designated as a National Historic Site of Japan.
It is the second-largest keyhole-shaped kofun on Iki Island after the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="Iki Tourism Navi Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.ikikankou.com/wp/?p=3831 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Iki City Tourism Federation "Iki Tourism Navi").</ref> It is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Overview ==
The kofun was constructed on a hill about 100 meters above sea level in the central part of Iki Island. The name "Tsushimazuka" is believed to derive from the view of {{ill|Tsushima Island|ja|Tsushima Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from the mound.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/240 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Education Bureau, "Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Encyclopedia").</ref> Investigations were conducted in the early Showa period by Tomoo Matsumoto, in 1981 by Yasuhiko Matsunaga, and in 1991 by the {{ill|Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|ja|長崎県教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Extensive excavations were carried out between 1997 and 2000.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Mound ==
The mound is keyhole-shaped with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * '''Total Length:''' About 65 meters * '''Rear Circular Mound:''' Approximately 36 meters in diameter, about 8 meters in height * '''Front Rectangular Mound:''' Approximately 25 meters wide
Similar to the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the front part of the mound is longer compared to the rear, which has steep slopes resembling an overturned bowl. The top of the rear mound was flattened during wartime to install anti-aircraft guns, giving the appearance of a two-tiered structure today. No evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (covering stones) or surrounding moats has been found. A small shrine is currently enshrined at the base of the front part of the mound.
== Burial Facility ==
The burial facility consists of a dual-chamber {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Historical records indicate that the stone chamber had already been opened during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and people could enter it{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
'''Stone Chamber Dimensions:''' * '''Total Length:''' 9.4 meters{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (11 meters<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>) * '''Main Chamber:''' Approximately 3.6 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 3.8 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' Approximately 3.9 meters long, 1.5 meters wide
== Artifacts ==
Artifacts discovered include gold items, gilt-bronze sword hilts, horse gear, dragonfly beads, pottery, and {{ill|Silla pottery|ja|新羅土器|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
<gallery> Tsushimazuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|Top of the rear circular mound </gallery>
== Related Facilities ==
* {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Located in Ashibe Town, Iki City)
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used in writing the article)}} * Published by local governments: ''' {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2017|chapter=|title=Iki City's Cultural Properties|publisher=Iki City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iki City's Cultural Properties|2017}}}} * Encyclopedias: ''' {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=Hajime Tagawa|year=2002|chapter=Tsushimazuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}}
== Further Reading == {{small|(Related literature not used in writing the article)}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/14748 |title=Detailed Distribution Survey Report of Prefectural Kofun - A Report on Mound and Stone Chamber Documentation-|series=Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Property Survey Report No. 106|publisher=Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=|title=Tsushimazuka Kofun - Excavation Survey in Connection with City Ruins Research Project-|series=Iki City Cultural Property Survey Report No. 6|publisher=Iki City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=|title=Ancient Tombs of Iki Island - Current Status Survey-|series=Iki City Cultural Property Survey Report No. 20|publisher=Iki City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsushimazuka Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Cluster] - Nagasaki Prefecture's official website on cultural properties * [[Sugoroku Kofun]] ...meters; the largest in Iki Islan ...<nowiki>{{ill|Tsushima-tsuka Kofun|ja|'''対馬塚古墳'''|qq=draft|qq=draft}}</nowiki>, followed by {{ill|Sugoroku Kofun|ja|'''双六古墳'''<nowiki>|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and l ...ngth: 91 meters; the largest in Iki Islan ...{{ill|Tsushima-tsuka Kofun|ja|'''対馬塚古墳'''|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, followed by {{ill|Sugoroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and later transitioned to ci ... 784 bytes (100 words) - 06:28, 19 January 2025 * [[Iki Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Tsushima-tsuka Kofun|ja|'''対馬塚古墳'''|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Keyhole-shaped mound (total length: 63 meters). ...fun Group began in the late 6th century with {{ill|Tsushima-tsuka Kofun|ja|'''対馬塚古墳'''|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, followed by {{ill|Sugoroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and later transitioned to ci ...
== 4 KB (487 words) - 06:01, 19 January 2025 == Sugoroku Kofun ...meters; the largest in Iki Islan ...{{ill|Tsushima-tsuka Kofun|ja|対馬塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, followed by {{ill|Sugoroku Kofun|ja|'''双六古墳'''|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and l ...ngth: 91 meters; the largest in Iki Islan ...{{ill|Tsushima-tsuka Kofun|ja|対馬塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, followed by {{ill|Sugoroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and later transitioned to ci ... 784 bytes (100 words) - 06:28, 19 January 2025 Iki Kofun Group * {{ill|Tsushima-tsuka Kofun|ja|対馬塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Keyhole-shaped mound (total length: 63 meters). ...fun Group began in the late 6th century with {{ill|Tsushima-tsuka Kofun|ja|対馬塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, followed by {{ill|Sugoroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and later transitioned to ci ... 4 KB (487 words) - 06:01, 19 January 2025

revision.text:
{{Infobox Kofun of Japan | Name = Tsushimazuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tsushimazuka Kofun, hokora.jpg|280px]] | Caption = Front part of the mound with stone shrine | Other Name = | Cluster = {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Location = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi | Latitude = 33|47|43.02 | Longitude = 129|41|31.62 | ISO = JP-42 | Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Size = 65m in length, 8m in height | Burial = Dual-chamber {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Artifacts = Gold items, gilt-bronze sword hilt, horse gear, dragonfly beads, pottery, Silla pottery | Satellite Mounds = | Built = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Burial Person = | Historic Site = Part of the National Historic Site "{{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" | Tangible Cultural Property = | Notes = | Map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Keyhole-shaped kofun-south | Label = Tsushimazuka Kofun | Label Position = bottom }}
'''Tsushimazuka Kofun''' (対馬塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its shape is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is one of the kofun that constitute the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, designated as a National Historic Site of Japan.
It is the second-largest keyhole-shaped kofun on Iki Island after the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="Iki Tourism Navi Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.ikikankou.com/wp/?p=3831 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Iki City Tourism Federation "Iki Tourism Navi").</ref> It is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Overview ==
The kofun was constructed on a hill about 100 meters above sea level in the central part of Iki Island. The name "Tsushimazuka" is believed to derive from the view of {{ill|Tsushima Island|ja|Tsushima Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from the mound.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/240 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Education Bureau, "Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Encyclopedia").</ref> Investigations were conducted in the early Showa period by Tomoo Matsumoto, in 1981 by Yasuhiko Matsunaga, and in 1991 by the {{ill|Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|ja|長崎県教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Extensive excavations were carried out between 1997 and 2000.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Mound ==
The mound is keyhole-shaped with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * '''Total Length:''' About 65 meters * '''Rear Circular Mound:''' Approximately 36 meters in diameter, about 8 meters in height * '''Front Rectangular Mound:''' Approximately 25 meters wide
Similar to the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the front part of the mound is longer compared to the rear, which has steep slopes resembling an overturned bowl. The top of the rear mound was flattened during wartime to install anti-aircraft guns, giving the appearance of a two-tiered structure today. No evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (covering stones) or surrounding moats has been found. A small shrine is currently enshrined at the base of the front part of the mound.
== Burial Facility ==
The burial facility consists of a dual-chamber {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Historical records indicate that the stone chamber had already been opened during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and people could enter it{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
'''Stone Chamber Dimensions:''' * '''Total Length:''' 9.4 meters{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (11 meters<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>) * '''Main Chamber:''' Approximately 3.6 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 3.8 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' Approximately 3.9 meters long, 1.5 meters wide
== Artifacts ==
Artifacts discovered include gold items, gilt-bronze sword hilts, horse gear, dragonfly beads, pottery, and {{ill|Silla pottery|ja|新羅土器|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
<gallery> Tsushimazuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|Top of the rear circular mound </gallery>
== Related Facilities ==
* {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Located in Ashibe Town, Iki City)

== 4 KB (487 words) - 06:01, 19 January 2025 == Sugoroku Kofun ...meters; the largest in Iki Islan ...{{ill|Tsushima-tsuka Kofun|ja|対馬塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, followed by {{ill|Sugoroku Kofun|ja|'''双六古墳'''|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and l ...ngth: 91 meters; the largest in Iki Islan ...{{ill|Tsushima-tsuka Kofun|ja|対馬塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, followed by {{ill|Sugoroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and later transitioned to ci ... 784 bytes (100 words) - 06:28, 19 January 2025 Iki Kofun Group * {{ill|Tsushima-tsuka Kofun|ja|対馬塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Keyhole-shaped mound (total length: 63 meters). ...fun Group began in the late 6th century with {{ill|Tsushima-tsuka Kofun|ja|対馬塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, followed by {{ill|Sugoroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and later transitioned to ci ... 4 KB (487 words) - 06:01, 19 January 2025

revision.text:
{{Infobox Kofun of Japan | Name = Tsushimazuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tsushimazuka Kofun, hokora.jpg|280px]] | Caption = Front part of the mound with stone shrine | Other Name = | Cluster = {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} | Location = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki, Nagasaki|qq=}} Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi | Latitude = 33|47|43.02 | Longitude = 129|41|31.62 | ISO = JP-42 | Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} | Size = 65m in length, 8m in height | Burial = Dual-chamber {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Artifacts = Gold items, gilt-bronze sword hilt, horse gear, dragonfly beads, pottery, Silla pottery | Satellite Mounds = | Built = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th century|qq=}} | Burial Person = | Historic Site = Part of the National Historic Site "{{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Cluster|qq=}}" | Tangible Cultural Property = | Notes = | Map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Keyhole-shaped kofun-south | Label = Tsushimazuka Kofun | Label Position = bottom }}
'''Tsushimazuka Kofun''' (対馬塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki, Nagasaki|qq=}}, Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=}}. Its shape is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}. It is one of the kofun that constitute the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, designated as a National Historic Site of Japan.
It is the second-largest keyhole-shaped kofun on Iki Island after the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|qq=}}.<ref name="Iki Tourism Navi Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.ikikankou.com/wp/?p=3831 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Iki City Tourism Federation "Iki Tourism Navi").</ref> It is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Overview ==
The kofun was constructed on a hill about 100 meters above sea level in the central part of Iki Island. The name "Tsushimazuka" is believed to derive from the view of {{ill|Tsushima Island|ja|Tsushima Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Island|qq=}} from the mound.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/240 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Education Bureau, "Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Encyclopedia").</ref> Investigations were conducted in the early Showa period by Tomoo Matsumoto, in 1981 by Yasuhiko Matsunaga, and in 1991 by the {{ill|Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|ja|長崎県教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|qq=}}. Extensive excavations were carried out between 1997 and 2000.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Mound ==
The mound is keyhole-shaped with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * '''Total Length:''' About 65 meters * '''Rear Circular Mound:''' Approximately 36 meters in diameter, about 8 meters in height * '''Front Rectangular Mound:''' Approximately 25 meters wide
Similar to the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|qq=}}, the front part of the mound is longer compared to the rear, which has steep slopes resembling an overturned bowl. The top of the rear mound was flattened during wartime to install anti-aircraft guns, giving the appearance of a two-tiered structure today. No evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}} (covering stones) or surrounding moats has been found. A small shrine is currently enshrined at the base of the front part of the mound.
== Burial Facility ==
The burial facility consists of a dual-chamber {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Historical records indicate that the stone chamber had already been opened during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}} and people could enter it{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
'''Stone Chamber Dimensions:''' * '''Total Length:''' 9.4 meters{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (11 meters<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>) * '''Main Chamber:''' Approximately 3.6 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 3.8 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' Approximately 3.9 meters long, 1.5 meters wide
== Artifacts ==
Artifacts discovered include gold items, gilt-bronze sword hilts, horse gear, dragonfly beads, pottery, and {{ill|Silla pottery|ja|新羅土器|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Silla pottery|qq=}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
<gallery> Tsushimazuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|Top of the rear circular mound </gallery>
== Related Facilities ==
* {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|qq=}} (Located in Ashibe Town, Iki City)
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used in writing the article)}} * Published by local governments: ''' {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2017|chapter=|title=Iki City's Cultural Properties|publisher=Iki City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iki City's Cultural Properties|2017}}}} * Encyclopedias: ''' {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=Hajime Tagawa|year=2002|chapter=Tsushimazuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Publishing|qq=}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}}

'''Tsushimazuka Kofun''' (対馬塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki, Nagasaki|qq=}}, Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=}}. Its shape is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}. It is one of the kofun that constitute the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, designated as a National Historic Site of Japan.
It is the second-largest keyhole-shaped kofun on Iki Island after the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|qq=}}.<ref name="Iki Tourism Navi Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.ikikankou.com/wp/?p=3831 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Iki City Tourism Federation "Iki Tourism Navi").</ref> It is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>

The kofun was constructed on a hill about 100 meters above sea level in the central part of Iki Island. The name "Tsushimazuka" is believed to derive from the view of {{ill|Tsushima Island|ja|Tsushima Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Island|qq=}} from the mound.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/240 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Education Bureau, "Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Encyclopedia").</ref> Investigations were conducted in the early Showa period by Tomoo Matsumoto, in 1981 by Yasuhiko Matsunaga, and in 1991 by the {{ill|Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|ja|長崎県教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|qq=}}. Extensive excavations were carried out between 1997 and 2000.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Mound ==
The mound is keyhole-shaped with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * '''Total Length:''' About 65 meters * '''Rear Circular Mound:''' Approximately 36 meters in diameter, about 8 meters in height * '''Front Rectangular Mound:''' Approximately 25 meters wide
Similar to the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|qq=}}, the front part of the mound is longer compared to the rear, which has steep slopes resembling an overturned bowl. The top of the rear mound was flattened during wartime to install anti-aircraft guns, giving the appearance of a two-tiered structure today. No evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}} (covering stones) or surrounding moats has been found. A small shrine is currently enshrined at the base of the front part of the mound.
== Burial Facility ==
The burial facility consists of a dual-chamber {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Historical records indicate that the stone chamber had already been opened during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}} and people could enter it{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
'''Stone Chamber Dimensions:''' * '''Total Length:''' 9.4 meters{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (11 meters<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>) * '''Main Chamber:''' Approximately 3.6 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 3.8 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' Approximately 3.9 meters long, 1.5 meters wide
== Artifacts ==
Artifacts discovered include gold items, gilt-bronze sword hilts, horse gear, dragonfly beads, pottery, and {{ill|Silla pottery|ja|新羅土器|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Silla pottery|qq=}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
<gallery> Tsushimazuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|Top of the rear circular mound </gallery>
== Related Facilities ==
* {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|qq=}} (Located in Ashibe Town, Iki City)
== References == {{reflist}}

Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} | Location = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki, Nagasaki|qq=}} Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi | Latitude = 33|47|43.02 | Longitude = 129|41|31.62 | ISO = JP-42 | Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} | Size = 65m in length, 8m in height | Burial = Dual-chamber {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Artifacts = Gold items, gilt-bronze sword hilt, horse gear, dragonfly beads, pottery, Silla pottery | Satellite Mounds = | Built = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th century|qq=}} | Burial Person = | Historic Site = Part of the National Historic Site "{{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Cluster|qq=}}" | Tangible Cultural Property = | Notes = | Map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Keyhole-shaped kofun-south | Label = Tsushimazuka Kofun | Label Position = bottom }}
'''Tsushimazuka Kofun''' (対馬塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki, Nagasaki|qq=}}, Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=}}. Its shape is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}. It is one of the kofun that constitute the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, designated as a National Historic Site of Japan.
It is the second-largest keyhole-shaped kofun on Iki Island after the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|qq=}}.<ref name="Iki Tourism Navi Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.ikikankou.com/wp/?p=3831 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Iki City Tourism Federation "Iki Tourism Navi").</ref> It is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Overview ==
The kofun was constructed on a hill about 100 meters above sea level in the central part of Iki Island. The name "Tsushimazuka" is believed to derive from the view of {{ill|Tsushima Island|ja|Tsushima Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Island|qq=}} from the mound.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/240 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Education Bureau, "Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Encyclopedia").</ref> Investigations were conducted in the early Showa period by Tomoo Matsumoto, in 1981 by Yasuhiko Matsunaga, and in 1991 by the {{ill|Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|ja|長崎県教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|qq=}}. Extensive excavations were carried out between 1997 and 2000.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Mound ==
The mound is keyhole-shaped with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * '''Total Length:''' About 65 meters * '''Rear Circular Mound:''' Approximately 36 meters in diameter, about 8 meters in height * '''Front Rectangular Mound:''' Approximately 25 meters wide
Similar to the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|qq=}}, the front part of the mound is longer compared to the rear, which has steep slopes resembling an overturned bowl. The top of the rear mound was flattened during wartime to install anti-aircraft guns, giving the appearance of a two-tiered structure today. No evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}} (covering stones) or surrounding moats has been found. A small shrine is currently enshrined at the base of the front part of the mound.
== Burial Facility ==
The burial facility consists of a dual-chamber {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Historical records indicate that the stone chamber had already been opened during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}} and people could enter it{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
'''Stone Chamber Dimensions:''' * '''Total Length:''' 9.4 meters{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (11 meters<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>) * '''Main Chamber:''' Approximately 3.6 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 3.8 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' Approximately 3.9 meters long, 1.5 meters wide
== Artifacts ==
Artifacts discovered include gold items, gilt-bronze sword hilts, horse gear, dragonfly beads, pottery, and {{ill|Silla pottery|ja|新羅土器|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Silla pottery|qq=}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
<gallery> Tsushimazuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|Top of the rear circular mound </gallery>
== Related Facilities ==
* {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|qq=}} (Located in Ashibe Town, Iki City)

ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} | Size = 65m in length, 8m in height | Burial = Dual-chamber {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone dragonfly beads, pottery, Silla pottery | Satellite Mounds = | Built = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th century|qq=}} | Burial Person = | Historic Site = Part of the National Historic Site "{{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Cluster|qq=}}" | Tangible Cultural Property = | Notes = | Map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Keyhole-shaped kofun-south | Label = Tsushimazuka Kofun | Label Position = bottom }}
'''Tsushimazuka Kofun''' (対馬塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki, Nagasaki|qq=}}, Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=}}. Its shape is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}. It is one of the kofun that constitute the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, designated as a National Historic Site of Japan.
It is the second-largest keyhole-shaped kofun on Iki Island after the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|qq=}}.<ref name="Iki Tourism Navi Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.ikikankou.com/wp/?p=3831 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Iki City Tourism Federation "Iki Tourism Navi").</ref> It is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Overview ==
The kofun was constructed on a hill about 100 meters above sea level in the central part of Iki Island. The name "Tsushimazuka" is believed to derive from the view of {{ill|Tsushima Island|ja|Tsushima Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Island|qq=}} from the mound.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/240 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Education Bureau, "Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Encyclopedia").</ref> Investigations were conducted in the early Showa period by Tomoo Matsumoto, in 1981 by Yasuhiko Matsunaga, and in 1991 by the {{ill|Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|ja|長崎県教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|qq=}}. Extensive excavations were carried out between 1997 and 2000.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Mound ==
The mound is keyhole-shaped with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * '''Total Length:''' About 65 meters * '''Rear Circular Mound:''' Approximately 36 meters in diameter, about 8 meters in height * '''Front Rectangular Mound:''' Approximately 25 meters wide
Similar to the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|qq=}}, the front part of the mound is longer compared to the rear, which has steep slopes resembling an overturned bowl. The top of the rear mound was flattened during wartime to install anti-aircraft guns, giving the appearance of a two-tiered structure today. No evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}} (covering stones) or surrounding moats has been found. A small shrine is currently enshrined at the base of the front part of the mound.
== Burial Facility ==
The burial facility consists of a dual-chamber {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Historical records indicate that the stone chamber had already been opened during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}} and people could enter it{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
'''Stone Chamber Dimensions:''' * '''Total Length:''' 9.4 meters{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (11 meters<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>) * '''Main Chamber:''' Approximately 3.6 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 3.8 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' Approximately 3.9 meters long, 1.5 meters wide
== Artifacts ==
Artifacts discovered include gold items, gilt-bronze sword hilts, horse gear, dragonfly beads, pottery, and {{ill|Silla pottery|ja|新羅土器|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Silla pottery|qq=}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
<gallery> Tsushimazuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|Top of the rear circular mound </gallery>
== Related Facilities ==
* {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|qq=}} (Located in Ashibe Town, Iki City)
