'''Tsushimazuka Kofun''' (対馬塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki, Nagasaki|qq=}}, Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=}}. Its shape is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}. It is one of the kofun that constitute the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, designated as a National Historic Site of Japan.
It is the second-largest keyhole-shaped kofun on Iki Island after the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|qq=}}.<ref name="Iki Tourism Navi Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.ikikankou.com/wp/?p=3831 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Iki City Tourism Federation "Iki Tourism Navi").</ref> It is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Overview ==
The kofun was constructed on a hill about 100 meters above sea level in the central part of Iki Island. The name "Tsushimazuka" is believed to derive from the view of {{ill|Tsushima Island|ja|Tsushima Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Island|qq=}} from the mound.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/240 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Education Bureau, "Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Encyclopedia").</ref> Investigations were conducted in the early Showa period by Tomoo Matsumoto, in 1981 by Yasuhiko Matsunaga, and in 1991 by the {{ill|Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|ja|長崎県教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|qq=}}. Extensive excavations were carried out between 1997 and 2000.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Mound ==
The mound is keyhole-shaped with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * '''Total Length:''' About 65 meters * '''Rear Circular Mound:''' Approximately 36 meters in diameter, about 8 meters in height * '''Front Rectangular Mound:''' Approximately 25 meters wide
Similar to the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|qq=}}, the front part of the mound is longer compared to the rear, which has steep slopes resembling an overturned bowl. The top of the rear mound was flattened during wartime to install anti-aircraft guns, giving the appearance of a two-tiered structure today. No evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}} (covering stones) or surrounding moats has been found. A small shrine is currently enshrined at the base of the front part of the mound.
== Burial Facility ==
The burial facility consists of a dual-chamber {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Historical records indicate that the stone chamber had already been opened during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}} and people could enter it{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
'''Stone Chamber Dimensions:''' * '''Total Length:''' 9.4 meters{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (11 meters<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>) * '''Main Chamber:''' Approximately 3.6 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 3.8 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' Approximately 3.9 meters long, 1.5 meters wide
== Artifacts ==
Artifacts discovered include gold items, gilt-bronze sword hilts, horse gear, dragonfly beads, pottery, and {{ill|Silla pottery|ja|新羅土器|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Silla pottery|qq=}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>

{{Infobox ancient site of Japan | Name = Tsushimazuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tsushimazuka Kofun, hokora.jpg|280px]] | Caption = Front part of the mound with stone shrine | Other Name = | Cluster = {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} | Location = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki, Nagasaki|qq=}} Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi | Latitude = 33|47|43.02 | Longitude = 129|41|31.62 | ISO = JP-42 | Type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} | Size = 65m in length, 8m in height | Burial = Dual-chamber {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Artifacts = Gold items, gilt-bronze sword hilt, horse gear, dragonfly beads, pottery, Silla pottery | Satellite Mounds = | Built = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th century|qq=}} | Burial Person = | Historic Site = Part of the National Historic Site "{{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Cluster|qq=}}" | Tangible Cultural Property = | Notes = | Map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Keyhole-shaped kofun-south | Label = Tsushimazuka Kofun | Label Position = bottom }}
'''Tsushimazuka Kofun''' (対馬塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki, Nagasaki|qq=}}, Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=}}. Its shape is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}. It is one of the kofun that constitute the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, designated as a National Historic Site of Japan.
It is the second-largest keyhole-shaped kofun on Iki Island after the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|qq=}}.<ref name="Iki Tourism Navi Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.ikikankou.com/wp/?p=3831 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Iki City Tourism Federation "Iki Tourism Navi").</ref> It is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Overview ==
The kofun was constructed on a hill about 100 meters above sea level in the central part of Iki Island. The name "Tsushimazuka" is believed to derive from the view of {{ill|Tsushima Island|ja|Tsushima Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Island|qq=}} from the mound.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/240 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Education Bureau, "Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Encyclopedia").</ref> Investigations were conducted in the early Showa period by Tomoo Matsumoto, in 1981 by Yasuhiko Matsunaga, and in 1991 by the {{ill|Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|ja|長崎県教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|qq=}}. Extensive excavations were carried out between 1997 and 2000.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Mound ==
The mound is keyhole-shaped with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * '''Total Length:''' About 65 meters * '''Rear Circular Mound:''' Approximately 36 meters in diameter, about 8 meters in height * '''Front Rectangular Mound:''' Approximately 25 meters wide
Similar to the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|qq=}}, the front part of the mound is longer compared to the rear, which has steep slopes resembling an overturned bowl. The top of the rear mound was flattened during wartime to install anti-aircraft guns, giving the appearance of a two-tiered structure today. No evidence of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}} (covering stones) or surrounding moats has been found. A small shrine is currently enshrined at the base of the front part of the mound.
== Burial Facility ==
The burial facility consists of a dual-chamber {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Historical records indicate that the stone chamber had already been opened during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}} and people could enter it{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
'''Stone Chamber Dimensions:''' * '''Total Length:''' 9.4 meters{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (11 meters<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>) * '''Main Chamber:''' Approximately 3.6 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 3.8 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' Approximately 3.9 meters long, 1.5 meters wide
== Artifacts ==
Artifacts discovered include gold items, gilt-bronze sword hilts, horse gear, dragonfly beads, pottery, and {{ill|Silla pottery|ja|新羅土器|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Silla pottery|qq=}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
<gallery> Tsushimazuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|Top of the rear circular mound </gallery>
== Related Facilities ==
* {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|qq=}} (Located in Ashibe Town, Iki City)
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used in writing the article)}} * Published by local governments: ''' {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2017|chapter=|title=Iki City's Cultural Properties|publisher=Iki City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iki City's Cultural Properties|2017}}}} * Encyclopedias: ''' {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=Hajime Tagawa|year=2002|chapter=Tsushimazuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Publishing|qq=}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}}
== Further Reading == {{small|(Related literature not used in writing the article)}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/14748 |title=Detailed Distribution Survey Report of Prefectural Kofun - A Report on Mound and Stone Chamber Documentation-|series=Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Property Survey Report No. 106|publisher=Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=|title=Tsushimazuka Kofun - Excavation Survey in Connection with City Ruins Research Project-|series=Iki City Cultural Property Survey Report No. 6|publisher=Iki City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=|title=Ancient Tombs of Iki Island - Current Status Survey-|series=Iki City Cultural Property Survey Report No. 20|publisher=Iki City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}}

'''Tsushimazuka Kofun''' (対馬塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]], Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi, [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]]. Its shape is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. It is one of the kofun that constitute the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, designated as a National Historic Site of Japan.
It is the second-largest keyhole-shaped kofun on Iki Island after the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|qq=}}.<ref name="Iki Tourism Navi Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.ikikankou.com/wp/?p=3831 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Iki City Tourism Federation "Iki Tourism Navi").</ref> It is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Overview ==
The kofun was constructed on a hill about 100 meters above sea level in the central part of Iki Island. The name "Tsushimazuka" is believed to derive from the view of {{ill|Tsushima Island|ja|Tsushima Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Island|qq=}} from the mound.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/240 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Education Bureau, "Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Encyclopedia").</ref> Investigations were conducted in the early Showa period by Tomoo Matsumoto, in 1981 by Yasuhiko Matsunaga, and in 1991 by the {{ill|Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|ja|長崎県教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|qq=}}. Extensive excavations were carried out between 1997 and 2000.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Mound ==
The mound is keyhole-shaped with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * '''Total Length:''' About 65 meters * '''Rear Circular Mound:''' Approximately 36 meters in diameter, about 8 meters in height * '''Front Rectangular Mound:''' Approximately 25 meters wide
Similar to the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|qq=}}, the front part of the mound is longer compared to the rear, which has steep slopes resembling an overturned bowl. The top of the rear mound was flattened during wartime to install anti-aircraft guns, giving the appearance of a two-tiered structure today. No evidence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (covering stones) or surrounding moats has been found. A small shrine is currently enshrined at the base of the front part of the mound.
== Burial Facility ==
The burial facility consists of a dual-chamber {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Historical records indicate that the stone chamber had already been opened during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and people could enter it{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
'''Stone Chamber Dimensions:''' * '''Total Length:''' 9.4 meters{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (11 meters<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>) * '''Main Chamber:''' Approximately 3.6 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 3.8 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' Approximately 3.9 meters long, 1.5 meters wide
== Artifacts ==
Artifacts discovered include gold items, gilt-bronze sword hilts, horse gear, dragonfly beads, pottery, and {{ill|Silla pottery|ja|新羅土器|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Silla pottery|qq=}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
<gallery> Tsushimazuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|Top of the rear circular mound </gallery>
== Related Facilities ==
* {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|qq=}} (Located in Ashibe Town, Iki City)
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used in writing the article)}} * Published by local governments: ''' {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2017|chapter=|title=Iki City's Cultural Properties|publisher=Iki City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iki City's Cultural Properties|2017}}}} * Encyclopedias: ''' {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=Hajime Tagawa|year=2002|chapter=Tsushimazuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Publishing|qq=}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}}
== Further Reading == {{small|(Related literature not used in writing the article)}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/14748 |title=Detailed Distribution Survey Report of Prefectural Kofun - A Report on Mound and Stone Chamber Documentation-|series=Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Property Survey Report No. 106|publisher=Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=|title=Tsushimazuka Kofun - Excavation Survey in Connection with City Ruins Research Project-|series=Iki City Cultural Property Survey Report No. 6|publisher=Iki City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=|title=Ancient Tombs of Iki Island - Current Status Survey-|series=Iki City Cultural Property Survey Report No. 20|publisher=Iki City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}}

{{Infobox ancient site of Japan | Name = Tsushimazuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tsushimazuka Kofun, hokora.jpg|280px]] | Caption = Front part of the mound with stone shrine | Other Name = | Cluster = {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} | Location = [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]] Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi | Latitude = 33|47|43.02 | Longitude = 129|41|31.62 | ISO = JP-42 | Type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | Size = 65m in length, 8m in height | Burial = Dual-chamber {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Artifacts = Gold items, gilt-bronze sword hilt, horse gear, dragonfly beads, pottery, Silla pottery | Satellite Mounds = | Built = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] | Burial Person = | Historic Site = Part of the National Historic Site "{{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Cluster|qq=}}" | Tangible Cultural Property = | Notes = | Map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Keyhole-shaped kofun-south | Label = Tsushimazuka Kofun | Label Position = bottom }}
'''Tsushimazuka Kofun''' (対馬塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]], Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi, [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]]. Its shape is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. It is one of the kofun that constitute the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, designated as a National Historic Site of Japan.
It is the second-largest keyhole-shaped kofun on Iki Island after the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|qq=}}.<ref name="Iki Tourism Navi Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.ikikankou.com/wp/?p=3831 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Iki City Tourism Federation "Iki Tourism Navi").</ref> It is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Overview ==
The kofun was constructed on a hill about 100 meters above sea level in the central part of Iki Island. The name "Tsushimazuka" is believed to derive from the view of {{ill|Tsushima Island|ja|Tsushima Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Island|qq=}} from the mound.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/240 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Education Bureau, "Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Encyclopedia").</ref> Investigations were conducted in the early Showa period by Tomoo Matsumoto, in 1981 by Yasuhiko Matsunaga, and in 1991 by the {{ill|Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|ja|長崎県教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|qq=}}. Extensive excavations were carried out between 1997 and 2000.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Mound ==
The mound is keyhole-shaped with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * '''Total Length:''' About 65 meters * '''Rear Circular Mound:''' Approximately 36 meters in diameter, about 8 meters in height * '''Front Rectangular Mound:''' Approximately 25 meters wide
Similar to the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|qq=}}, the front part of the mound is longer compared to the rear, which has steep slopes resembling an overturned bowl. The top of the rear mound was flattened during wartime to install anti-aircraft guns, giving the appearance of a two-tiered structure today. No evidence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (covering stones) or surrounding moats has been found. A small shrine is currently enshrined at the base of the front part of the mound.
== Burial Facility ==
The burial facility consists of a dual-chamber {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Historical records indicate that the stone chamber had already been opened during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and people could enter it{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
'''Stone Chamber Dimensions:''' * '''Total Length:''' 9.4 meters{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (11 meters<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>) * '''Main Chamber:''' Approximately 3.6 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 3.8 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' Approximately 3.9 meters long, 1.5 meters wide
== Artifacts ==
Artifacts discovered include gold items, gilt-bronze sword hilts, horse gear, dragonfly beads, pottery, and {{ill|Silla pottery|ja|新羅土器|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Silla pottery|qq=}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
<gallery> Tsushimazuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|Top of the rear circular mound </gallery>
== Related Facilities ==
* {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|qq=}} (Located in Ashibe Town, Iki City)

{{Infobox ancient site of Japan | Name = Tsushimazuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tsushimazuka Kofun, hokora.jpg|280px]] | Caption = Front part of the mound with stone shrine | Other Name = | Cluster = {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} | Location = [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]] Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi | Latitude = 33|47|43.02 | Longitude = 129|41|31.62 | ISO = JP-42 | Type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | Size = 65m in length, 8m in height | Burial = Dual-chamber {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Artifacts = Gold items, gilt-bronze sword hilt, horse gear, dragonfly beads, pottery, Silla pottery | Satellite Mounds = | Built = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] | Burial Person = | Historic Site = Part of the National Historic Site "{{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Cluster|qq=}}" | Tangible Cultural Property = | Notes = | Map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Keyhole-shaped kofun-south | Label = Tsushimazuka Kofun | Label Position = bottom }}
'''Tsushimazuka Kofun''' (対馬塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]], Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi, [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]]. Its shape is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. It is one of the kofun that constitute the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, designated as a National Historic Site of Japan.
It is the second-largest keyhole-shaped kofun on Iki Island after the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|qq=}}.<ref name="Iki Tourism Navi Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.ikikankou.com/wp/?p=3831 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Iki City Tourism Federation "Iki Tourism Navi").</ref> It is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Overview ==
The kofun was constructed on a hill about 100 meters above sea level in the central part of Iki Island. The name "Tsushimazuka" is believed to derive from the view of {{ill|Tsushima Island|ja|Tsushima Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Island|qq=}} from the mound.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/240 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Education Bureau, "Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Encyclopedia").</ref> Investigations were conducted in the early Showa period by Tomoo Matsumoto, in 1981 by Yasuhiko Matsunaga, and in 1991 by the {{ill|Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|ja|長崎県教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|qq=}}. Extensive excavations were carried out between 1997 and 2000.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Mound ==
The mound is keyhole-shaped with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * '''Total Length:''' About 65 meters * '''Rear Circular Mound:''' Approximately 36 meters in diameter, about 8 meters in height * '''Front Rectangular Mound:''' Approximately 25 meters wide
Similar to the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|qq=}}, the front part of the mound is longer compared to the rear, which has steep slopes resembling an overturned bowl. The top of the rear mound was flattened during wartime to install anti-aircraft guns, giving the appearance of a two-tiered structure today. No evidence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (covering stones) or surrounding moats has been found. A small shrine is currently enshrined at the base of the front part of the mound.
== Burial Facility ==
The burial facility consists of a dual-chamber {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Historical records indicate that the stone chamber had already been opened during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and people could enter it{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
'''Stone Chamber Dimensions:''' * '''Total Length:''' 9.4 meters{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (11 meters<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>) * '''Main Chamber:''' Approximately 3.6 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 3.8 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' Approximately 3.9 meters long, 1.5 meters wide
== Artifacts ==
Artifacts discovered include gold items, gilt-bronze sword hilts, horse gear, dragonfly beads, pottery, and {{ill|Silla pottery|ja|新羅土器|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Silla pottery|qq=}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
<gallery> Tsushimazuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|Top of the rear circular mound </gallery>
== Related Facilities ==
* {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|qq=}} (Located in Ashibe Town, Iki City)
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used in writing the article)}} * Published by local governments: ''' {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2017|chapter=|title=Iki City's Cultural Properties|publisher=Iki City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iki City's Cultural Properties|2017}}}} * Encyclopedias: ''' {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=Hajime Tagawa|year=2002|chapter=Tsushimazuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Publishing|qq=}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}}
== Further Reading == {{small|(Related literature not used in writing the article)}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/14748 |title=Detailed Distribution Survey Report of Prefectural Kofun - A Report on Mound and Stone Chamber Documentation-|series=Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Property Survey Report No. 106|publisher=Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=|title=Tsushimazuka Kofun - Excavation Survey in Connection with City Ruins Research Project-|series=Iki City Cultural Property Survey Report No. 6|publisher=Iki City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=|title=Ancient Tombs of Iki Island - Current Status Survey-|series=Iki City Cultural Property Survey Report No. 20|publisher=Iki City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}}

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:長崎県の古墳
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site of Japan | Name = Tsushimazuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tsushimazuka Kofun, hokora.jpg|280px]] | Caption = Front part of the mound with stone shrine | Other Name = | Cluster = {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} | Location = [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]] Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi | Latitude = 33|47|43.02 | Longitude = 129|41|31.62 | ISO = JP-42 | Type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | Size = 65m in length, 8m in height | Burial = Dual-chamber {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Artifacts = Gold items, gilt-bronze sword hilt, horse gear, dragonfly beads, pottery, Silla pottery | Satellite Mounds = | Built = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] | Burial Person = | Historic Site = Part of the National Historic Site "{{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Cluster|qq=}}" | Tangible Cultural Property = | Notes = | Map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Keyhole-shaped kofun-south | Label = Tsushimazuka Kofun | Label Position = bottom }}
'''Tsushimazuka Kofun''' (対馬塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]], Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi, [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]]. Its shape is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. It is one of the kofun that constitute the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, designated as a National Historic Site of Japan.
It is the second-largest keyhole-shaped kofun on Iki Island after the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|qq=}}.<ref name="Iki Tourism Navi Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.ikikankou.com/wp/?p=3831 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Iki City Tourism Federation "Iki Tourism Navi").</ref> It is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Overview ==
The kofun was constructed on a hill about 100 meters above sea level in the central part of Iki Island. The name "Tsushimazuka" is believed to derive from the view of {{ill|Tsushima Island|ja|Tsushima Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Island|qq=}} from the mound.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/240 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Education Bureau, "Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Encyclopedia").</ref> Investigations were conducted in the early Showa period by Tomoo Matsumoto, in 1981 by Yasuhiko Matsunaga, and in 1991 by the {{ill|Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|ja|長崎県教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|qq=}}. Extensive excavations were carried out between 1997 and 2000.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Mound ==
The mound is keyhole-shaped with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * '''Total Length:''' About 65 meters * '''Rear Circular Mound:''' Approximately 36 meters in diameter, about 8 meters in height * '''Front Rectangular Mound:''' Approximately 25 meters wide
Similar to the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|qq=}}, the front part of the mound is longer compared to the rear, which has steep slopes resembling an overturned bowl. The top of the rear mound was flattened during wartime to install anti-aircraft guns, giving the appearance of a two-tiered structure today. No evidence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (covering stones) or surrounding moats has been found. A small shrine is currently enshrined at the base of the front part of the mound.
== Burial Facility ==
The burial facility consists of a dual-chamber {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Historical records indicate that the stone chamber had already been opened during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and people could enter it{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
'''Stone Chamber Dimensions:''' * '''Total Length:''' 9.4 meters{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (11 meters<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>) * '''Main Chamber:''' Approximately 3.6 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 3.8 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' Approximately 3.9 meters long, 1.5 meters wide
== Artifacts ==
Artifacts discovered include gold items, gilt-bronze sword hilts, horse gear, dragonfly beads, pottery, and {{ill|Silla pottery|ja|新羅土器|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Silla pottery|qq=}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
<gallery> Tsushimazuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|Top of the rear circular mound </gallery>
== Related Facilities ==
* {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|qq=}} (Located in Ashibe Town, Iki City)
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used in writing the article)}} * Published by local governments: ''' {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2017|chapter=|title=Iki City's Cultural Properties|publisher=Iki City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iki City's Cultural Properties|2017}}}} * Encyclopedias: ''' {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=Hajime Tagawa|year=2002|chapter=Tsushimazuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Publishing|qq=}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}}
== Further Reading == {{small|(Related literature not used in writing the article)}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/14748 |title=Detailed Distribution Survey Report of Prefectural Kofun - A Report on Mound and Stone Chamber Documentation-|series=Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Property Survey Report No. 106|publisher=Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=|title=Tsushimazuka Kofun - Excavation Survey in Connection with City Ruins Research Project-|series=Iki City Cultural Property Survey Report No. 6|publisher=Iki City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=|title=Ancient Tombs of Iki Island - Current Status Survey-|series=Iki City Cultural Property Survey Report No. 20|publisher=Iki City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}}

No evidence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (covering stones) or surrounding moats has been found. A small shrine is currently enshrined at the base of the front part of the mound.
== Burial Facility ==
The burial facility consists of a dual-chamber {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Historical records indicate that the stone chamber had already been opened during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and people could enter it{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
'''Stone Chamber Dimensions:''' * '''Total Length:''' 9.4 meters{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (11 meters<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>) * '''Main Chamber:''' Approximately 3.6 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 3.8 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' Approximately 3.9 meters long, 1.5 meters wide
== Artifacts ==
Artifacts discovered include gold items, gilt-bronze sword hilts, horse gear, dragonfly beads, pottery, and {{ill|Silla pottery|ja|新羅土器|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Silla pottery|qq=}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
<gallery> Tsushimazuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|Top of the rear circular mound </gallery>
== Related Facilities ==
* {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|qq=}} (Located in Ashibe Town, Iki City)

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site of Japan | Name = Tsushimazuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tsushimazuka Kofun, hokora.jpg|280px]] | Caption = Front part of the mound with stone shrine | Other Name = | Cluster = {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} | Location = [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]] Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi | Latitude = 33|47|43.02 | Longitude = 129|41|31.62 | ISO = JP-42 | Type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | Size = 65m in length, 8m in height | Burial = Dual-chamber {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Artifacts = Gold items, gilt-bronze sword hilt, horse gear, dragonfly beads, pottery, Silla pottery | Satellite Mounds = | Built = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] | Burial Person = | Historic Site = Part of the National Historic Site "{{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Cluster|qq=}}" | Tangible Cultural Property = | Notes = | Map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Keyhole-shaped kofun-south | Label = Tsushimazuka Kofun | Label Position = bottom }}
'''Tsushimazuka Kofun''' (対馬塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]], Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi, [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]]. Its shape is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. It is one of the kofun that constitute the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, designated as a National Historic Site of Japan.
It is the second-largest keyhole-shaped kofun on Iki Island after the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|qq=}}.<ref name="Iki Tourism Navi Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.ikikankou.com/wp/?p=3831 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Iki City Tourism Federation "Iki Tourism Navi").</ref> It is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Overview ==
The kofun was constructed on a hill about 100 meters above sea level in the central part of Iki Island. The name "Tsushimazuka" is believed to derive from the view of {{ill|Tsushima Island|ja|Tsushima Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Island|qq=}} from the mound.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/240 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Education Bureau, "Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Encyclopedia").</ref> Investigations were conducted in the early Showa period by Tomoo Matsumoto, in 1981 by Yasuhiko Matsunaga, and in 1991 by the {{ill|Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|ja|長崎県教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|qq=}}. Extensive excavations were carried out between 1997 and 2000.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Mound ==
The mound is keyhole-shaped with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * '''Total Length:''' About 65 meters * '''Rear Circular Mound:''' Approximately 36 meters in diameter, about 8 meters in height * '''Front Rectangular Mound:''' Approximately 25 meters wide
Similar to the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|qq=}}, the front part of the mound is longer compared to the rear, which has steep slopes resembling an overturned bowl. The top of the rear mound was flattened during wartime to install anti-aircraft guns, giving the appearance of a two-tiered structure today. No evidence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (covering stones) or surrounding moats has been found. A small shrine is currently enshrined at the base of the front part of the mound.
== Burial Facility ==
The burial facility consists of a dual-chamber {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Historical records indicate that the stone chamber had already been opened during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and people could enter it{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
'''Stone Chamber Dimensions:''' * '''Total Length:''' 9.4 meters{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (11 meters<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>) * '''Main Chamber:''' Approximately 3.6 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 3.8 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' Approximately 3.9 meters long, 1.5 meters wide
== Artifacts ==
Artifacts discovered include gold items, gilt-bronze sword hilts, horse gear, dragonfly beads, pottery, and {{ill|Silla pottery|ja|新羅土器|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Silla pottery|qq=}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
<gallery> Tsushimazuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|Top of the rear circular mound </gallery>
== Related Facilities ==
* {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|qq=}} (Located in Ashibe Town, Iki City)

'''Tsushimazuka Kofun''' (対馬塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]], Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi, [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]]. Its shape is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. It is one of the kofun that constitute the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, designated as a National Historic Site of Japan.
It is the second-largest keyhole-shaped kofun on Iki Island after the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|qq=}}.<ref name="Iki Tourism Navi Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.ikikankou.com/wp/?p=3831 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Iki City Tourism Federation "Iki Tourism Navi").</ref> It is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Overview ==
The kofun was constructed on a hill about 100 meters above sea level in the central part of Iki Island. The name "Tsushimazuka" is believed to derive from the view of {{ill|Tsushima Island|ja|Tsushima Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Island|qq=}} from the mound.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/240 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Education Bureau, "Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Encyclopedia").</ref> Investigations were conducted in the early Showa period by Tomoo Matsumoto, in 1981 by Yasuhiko Matsunaga, and in 1991 by the {{ill|Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|ja|長崎県教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|qq=}}. Extensive excavations were carried out between 1997 and 2000.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Mound ==
The mound is keyhole-shaped with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * '''Total Length:''' About 65 meters * '''Rear Circular Mound:''' Approximately 36 meters in diameter, about 8 meters in height * '''Front Rectangular Mound:''' Approximately 25 meters wide
Similar to the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|qq=}}, the front part of the mound is longer compared to the rear, which has steep slopes resembling an overturned bowl. The top of the rear mound was flattened during wartime to install anti-aircraft guns, giving the appearance of a two-tiered structure today. No evidence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (covering stones) or surrounding moats has been found. A small shrine is currently enshrined at the base of the front part of the mound.
== Burial Facility ==
The burial facility consists of a dual-chamber {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Historical records indicate that the stone chamber had already been opened during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and people could enter it{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
'''Stone Chamber Dimensions:''' * '''Total Length:''' 9.4 meters{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (11 meters<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>) * '''Main Chamber:''' Approximately 3.6 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 3.8 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' Approximately 3.9 meters long, 1.5 meters wide
== Artifacts ==
Artifacts discovered include gold items, gilt-bronze sword hilts, horse gear, dragonfly beads, pottery, and {{ill|Silla pottery|ja|新羅土器|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Silla pottery|qq=}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
<gallery> Tsushimazuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|Top of the rear circular mound </gallery>
== Related Facilities ==
* {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|qq=}} (Located in Ashibe Town, Iki City)

'''Tsushimazuka Kofun''' (対馬塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]], Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi, [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]]. Its shape is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. It is one of the kofun that constitute the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, designated as a National Historic Site of Japan.
It is the second-largest keyhole-shaped kofun on Iki Island after the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|qq=}}.<ref name="Iki Tourism Navi Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.ikikankou.com/wp/?p=3831 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Iki City Tourism Federation "Iki Tourism Navi").</ref> It is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Overview ==
The kofun was constructed on a hill about 100 meters above sea level in the central part of Iki Island. The name "Tsushimazuka" is believed to derive from the view of {{ill|Tsushima Island|ja|Tsushima Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Island|qq=}} from the mound.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/240 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Education Bureau, "Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Encyclopedia").</ref> Investigations were conducted in the early Showa period by Tomoo Matsumoto, in 1981 by Yasuhiko Matsunaga, and in 1991 by the {{ill|Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|ja|長崎県教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|qq=}}. Extensive excavations were carried out between 1997 and 2000.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Mound ==
The mound is keyhole-shaped with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * '''Total Length:''' About 65 meters * '''Rear Circular Mound:''' Approximately 36 meters in diameter, about 8 meters in height * '''Front Rectangular Mound:''' Approximately 25 meters wide
Similar to the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|qq=}}, the front part of the mound is longer compared to the rear, which has steep slopes resembling an overturned bowl. The top of the rear mound was flattened during wartime to install anti-aircraft guns, giving the appearance of a two-tiered structure today. No evidence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (covering stones) or surrounding moats has been found. A small shrine is currently enshrined at the base of the front part of the mound.
== Burial Facility ==
The burial facility consists of a dual-chamber {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Historical records indicate that the stone chamber had already been opened during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and people could enter it{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
'''Stone Chamber Dimensions:''' * '''Total Length:''' 9.4 meters{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (11 meters<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>) * '''Main Chamber:''' Approximately 3.6 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 3.8 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' Approximately 3.9 meters long, 1.5 meters wide
== Artifacts ==
Artifacts discovered include gold items, gilt-bronze sword hilts, horse gear, dragonfly beads, pottery, and {{ill|Silla pottery|ja|新羅土器|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Silla pottery|qq=}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site of Japan | Name = Tsushimazuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tsushimazuka Kofun, hokora.jpg|280px]] | Caption = Front part of the mound with stone shrine | Other Name = | Cluster = {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} | Location = [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]] Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi | Latitude = 33|47|43.02 | Longitude = 129|41|31.62 | ISO = JP-42 | Type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | Size = 65m in length, 8m in height | Burial = Dual-chamber {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Artifacts = Gold items, gilt-bronze sword hilt, horse gear, dragonfly beads, pottery, Silla pottery | Satellite Mounds = | Built = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] | Burial Person = | Historic Site = Part of the National Historic Site "{{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Cluster|qq=}}" | Tangible Cultural Property = | Notes = | Map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Keyhole-shaped kofun-south | Label = Tsushimazuka Kofun | Label Position = bottom }}
'''Tsushimazuka Kofun''' (対馬塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]], Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi, [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]]. Its shape is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. It is one of the kofun that constitute the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, designated as a National Historic Site of Japan.
It is the second-largest keyhole-shaped kofun on Iki Island after the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|qq=}}.<ref name="Iki Tourism Navi Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.ikikankou.com/wp/?p=3831 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Iki City Tourism Federation "Iki Tourism Navi").</ref> It is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Overview ==
The kofun was constructed on a hill about 100 meters above sea level in the central part of Iki Island. The name "Tsushimazuka" is believed to derive from the view of {{ill|Tsushima Island|ja|Tsushima Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Island|qq=}} from the mound.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/240 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Education Bureau, "Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Encyclopedia").</ref> Investigations were conducted in the early Showa period by Tomoo Matsumoto, in 1981 by Yasuhiko Matsunaga, and in 1991 by the {{ill|Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|ja|長崎県教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|qq=}}. Extensive excavations were carried out between 1997 and 2000.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Mound ==
The mound is keyhole-shaped with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * '''Total Length:''' About 65 meters * '''Rear Circular Mound:''' Approximately 36 meters in diameter, about 8 meters in height * '''Front Rectangular Mound:''' Approximately 25 meters wide
Similar to the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|qq=}}, the front part of the mound is longer compared to the rear, which has steep slopes resembling an overturned bowl. The top of the rear mound was flattened during wartime to install anti-aircraft guns, giving the appearance of a two-tiered structure today. No evidence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (covering stones) or surrounding moats has been found. A small shrine is currently enshrined at the base of the front part of the mound.
== Burial Facility ==
The burial facility consists of a dual-chamber {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Historical records indicate that the stone chamber had already been opened during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and people could enter it{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
'''Stone Chamber Dimensions:''' * '''Total Length:''' 9.4 meters{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (11 meters<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>) * '''Main Chamber:''' Approximately 3.6 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 3.8 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' Approximately 3.9 meters long, 1.5 meters wide
== Artifacts ==
Artifacts discovered include gold items, gilt-bronze sword hilts, horse gear, dragonfly beads, pottery, and {{ill|Silla pottery|ja|新羅土器|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Silla pottery|qq=}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
<gallery> Tsushimazuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|Top of the rear circular mound </gallery>
== Related Facilities ==
* {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|qq=}} (Located in Ashibe Town, Iki City)
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used in writing the article)}} * Published by local governments: ''' {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2017|chapter=|title=Iki City's Cultural Properties|publisher=Iki City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Iki City's Cultural Properties|2017}}}} * Encyclopedias: ''' {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=Hajime Tagawa|year=2002|chapter=Tsushimazuka Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Publishing|qq=}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}}
== Further Reading == {{small|(Related literature not used in writing the article)}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/14748 |title=Detailed Distribution Survey Report of Prefectural Kofun - A Report on Mound and Stone Chamber Documentation-|series=Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Property Survey Report No. 106|publisher=Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=|title=Tsushimazuka Kofun - Excavation Survey in Connection with City Ruins Research Project-|series=Iki City Cultural Property Survey Report No. 6|publisher=Iki City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=|title=Ancient Tombs of Iki Island - Current Status Survey-|series=Iki City Cultural Property Survey Report No. 20|publisher=Iki City Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site of Japan | Name = Tsushimazuka Kofun | image = [[File:Tsushimazuka Kofun, hokora.jpg|280px]] | Caption = Front part of the mound with stone shrine | Other Name = | Cluster = {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} | Location = [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]] Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi | Latitude = 33|47|43.02 | Longitude = 129|41|31.62 | ISO = JP-42 | Type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | Size = 65m in length, 8m in height | Burial = Dual-chamber {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Artifacts = Gold items, gilt-bronze sword hilt, horse gear, dragonfly beads, pottery, Silla pottery | Satellite Mounds = | Built = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] | Burial Person = | Historic Site = Part of the National Historic Site "{{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Cluster|qq=}}" | Tangible Cultural Property = | Notes = | Map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Keyhole-shaped kofun-south | Label = Tsushimazuka Kofun | Label Position = bottom }}
'''Tsushimazuka Kofun''' (対馬塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]], Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi, [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]]. Its shape is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. It is one of the kofun that constitute the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, designated as a National Historic Site of Japan.
It is the second-largest keyhole-shaped kofun on Iki Island after the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|qq=}}.<ref name="Iki Tourism Navi Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.ikikankou.com/wp/?p=3831 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Iki City Tourism Federation "Iki Tourism Navi").</ref> It is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Overview ==
The kofun was constructed on a hill about 100 meters above sea level in the central part of Iki Island. The name "Tsushimazuka" is believed to derive from the view of {{ill|Tsushima Island|ja|Tsushima Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Island|qq=}} from the mound.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/240 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Education Bureau, "Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Encyclopedia").</ref> Investigations were conducted in the early Showa period by Tomoo Matsumoto, in 1981 by Yasuhiko Matsunaga, and in 1991 by the {{ill|Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|ja|長崎県教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|qq=}}. Extensive excavations were carried out between 1997 and 2000.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Mound ==
The mound is keyhole-shaped with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * '''Total Length:''' About 65 meters * '''Rear Circular Mound:''' Approximately 36 meters in diameter, about 8 meters in height * '''Front Rectangular Mound:''' Approximately 25 meters wide
Similar to the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|qq=}}, the front part of the mound is longer compared to the rear, which has steep slopes resembling an overturned bowl. The top of the rear mound was flattened during wartime to install anti-aircraft guns, giving the appearance of a two-tiered structure today. No evidence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (covering stones) or surrounding moats has been found. A small shrine is currently enshrined at the base of the front part of the mound.
== Burial Facility ==
The burial facility consists of a dual-chamber {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Historical records indicate that the stone chamber had already been opened during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and people could enter it{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
'''Stone Chamber Dimensions:''' * '''Total Length:''' 9.4 meters{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (11 meters<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>) * '''Main Chamber:''' Approximately 3.6 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 3.8 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' Approximately 3.9 meters long, 1.5 meters wide
== Artifacts ==
Artifacts discovered include gold items, gilt-bronze sword hilts, horse gear, dragonfly beads, pottery, and {{ill|Silla pottery|ja|新羅土器|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Silla pottery|qq=}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
<gallery> Tsushimazuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|Top of the rear circular mound </gallery>
== Related Facilities ==
* {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|qq=}} (Located in Ashibe Town, Iki City)

'''Tsushimazuka Kofun''' (対馬塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]], Katsumoto Town, Tategami Higashi, [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]]. Its shape is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]. It is one of the kofun that constitute the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, designated as a National Historic Site of Japan.
It is the second-largest keyhole-shaped kofun on Iki Island after the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|qq=}}.<ref name="Iki Tourism Navi Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.ikikankou.com/wp/?p=3831 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Iki City Tourism Federation "Iki Tourism Navi").</ref> It is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>

The kofun was constructed on a hill about 100 meters above sea level in the central part of Iki Island. The name "Tsushimazuka" is believed to derive from the view of {{ill|Tsushima Island|ja|Tsushima Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Island|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from the mound.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun">[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/240 Tsushimazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Education Bureau, "Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Encyclopedia").</ref> Investigations were conducted in the early Showa period by Tomoo Matsumoto, in 1981 by Yasuhiko Matsunaga, and in 1991 by the {{ill|Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|ja|長崎県教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education|qq=}}. Extensive excavations were carried out between 1997 and 2000.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
== Mound ==
The mound is keyhole-shaped with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * '''Total Length:''' About 65 meters * '''Rear Circular Mound:''' Approximately 36 meters in diameter, about 8 meters in height * '''Front Rectangular Mound:''' Approximately 25 meters wide
Similar to the {{ill|Sōroku Kofun|ja|双六古墳|1=Sōroku Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōroku Kofun|qq=}}, the front part of the mound is longer compared to the rear, which has steep slopes resembling an overturned bowl. The top of the rear mound was flattened during wartime to install anti-aircraft guns, giving the appearance of a two-tiered structure today. No evidence of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (covering stones) or surrounding moats has been found. A small shrine is currently enshrined at the base of the front part of the mound.
== Burial Facility ==
The burial facility consists of a dual-chamber {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passage{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Historical records indicate that the stone chamber had already been opened during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] and people could enter it{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
'''Stone Chamber Dimensions:''' * '''Total Length:''' 9.4 meters{{Sfn|Tsushimazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (11 meters<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>) * '''Main Chamber:''' Approximately 3.6 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 3.8 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' Approximately 3.9 meters long, 1.5 meters wide
== Artifacts ==
Artifacts discovered include gold items, gilt-bronze sword hilts, horse gear, dragonfly beads, pottery, and {{ill|Silla pottery|ja|新羅土器|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Silla pottery|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="Nagasaki Prefecture Ruins Tsushimazuka Kofun"/>
<gallery> Tsushimazuka Kofun, funchou.jpg|Top of the rear circular mound </gallery>
== Related Facilities ==
* {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|qq=}} (Located in Ashibe Town, Iki City)
